source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure ‘Skip_show_database’ Database Flag for Cloud SQL MySQL Instance Is Set to ‘On’

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure ‘Skip_show_database’ Database Flag for Cloud SQL MySQL Instance Is Set to ‘On’
input_row.description: It is recommended to set skip_show_database database flag for Cloud SQL Mysql instance to on
input_row.rationale: skip_show_database database flag prevents people from using the SHOW DATABASES statement if they do not have the SHOW DATABASES privilege. This can improve security if you have concerns about users being able to see databases belonging to other users. Its effect depends on the SHOW DATABASES privilege: If the variable value is ON, the SHOW DATABASES statement is permitted only to users who have the SHOW DATABASES privilege, and the statement displays all database names. If the value is OFF, SHOW DATABASES is permitted to all users, but displays the names of only those databases for which the user has the SHOW DATABASES or other privilege. This recommendation is applicable to Mysql database instances.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Select the instance to open its Instance Overview page 3. Ensure the database flag skip_show_database that has been set is listed under the Database flags section. From Google Cloud CLI 1. List all Cloud SQL database Instances gcloud sql instances list 2. Ensure the below command returns on for every Cloud SQL Mysql database instance gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> --format=json | jq '.settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="skip_show_database")|.value'
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Select the Mysql instance for which you want to enable to database flag. 3. Click Edit. 4. Scroll down to the Flags section. 5. To set a flag that has not been set on the instance before, click Add a Database Flag, choose the flag skip_show_database from the drop-down menu, and set its value to on. 6. Click Save to save your changes. 7. Confirm your changes under Flags on the Overview page. From Google Cloud CLI 1. List all Cloud SQL database Instances gcloud sql instances list 2. Configure the skip_show_database database flag for every Cloud SQL Mysql database instance using the below command. gcloud sql instances patch <INSTANCE_NAME> --database-flags skip_show_database=on Note : This command will overwrite all database flags previously set. To keep those and add new ones, include the values for all flags you want set on the instance; any flag not specifically included is set to its default value. For flags that do not take a value, specify the flag name followed by an equals sign ("="). References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/mysql/flags 2. https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/5.7/en/server-system- variables.html#sysvar_skip_show_database Additional Information: WARNING: This patch modifies database flag values, which may require your instance to be restarted. Check the list of supported flags - https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/mysql/flags - to see if your instance will be restarted when this patch is submitted. Note : some database flag settings can affect instance availability or stability, and remove the instance from the Cloud SQL SLA. For information about these flags, see Operational Guidelines." Note : Configuring the above flag restarts the Cloud SQL instance.
input_row.references: 1. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/mysql/flags 2. https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/5.7/en/server-system- variables.html#sysvar_skip_show_database Additional Information: WARNING: This patch modifies database flag values, which may require your instance to be restarted. Check the list of supported flags - https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/mysql/flags - to see if your instance will be restarted when this patch is submitted. Note : some database flag settings can affect instance availability or stability, and remove the instance from the Cloud SQL SLA. For information about these flags, see Operational Guidelines." Note : Configuring the above flag restarts the Cloud SQL instance.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control can be fully verified using GCP APIs and Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands. The `audit` section provides detailed CLI commands to check if the `skip_show_database` flag is set to `on` for Cloud SQL MySQL instances. This aligns with the decision criteria that emphasize the availability of GCP CLI commands for verification, indicating an automated approach.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_sql_instance_skip_show_database_flag_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP CLI Command:** Use `gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME>` to retrieve the configuration details of the Cloud SQL instances.
- **JQ Command:** Parse the JSON output using `jq` to check for the `skip_show_database` database flag and ensure it is set to `on`.
- **Resources to Enumerate:** All Cloud SQL MySQL instances in the GCP project.
- **Configuration Property to Validate:** `.settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="skip_show_database")|.value` should equal `on`.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:** Pass if the flag value is `on` for all MySQL instances; Fail if the flag is not set or set to `off`.

By leveraging the available CLI commands, the program will validate that the necessary security configuration is enforced across all relevant resources.
step2_review:
---

### 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: **AGREE**

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:**  
  The classification as "Automated" is correct. The control can be fully validated using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`). There is no need for manual review, as all required information is accessible via the GCP CLI and API.

- **GCP SDK Capabilities:**  
  The `gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME>` command retrieves all instance settings, including `databaseFlags`. This information can be parsed programmatically for compliance checks.

- **Alternative GCP Methods:**  
  The same information is available via the [Cloud SQL Admin API](https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/mysql/admin-api/rest/v1beta4/instances/get), which returns the `settings.databaseFlags` array for each instance. This could be leveraged for more scalable, API-driven automation.

- **No GCP Config Equivalent:**  
  As of this review, GCP Config does not natively support this specific flag check for Cloud SQL.

---

### 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: **Score: 9/10**

- **Automation Details:**  
  - The use of `gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME>` is correct.
  - Parsing with `jq` for `.settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="skip_show_database") | .value` is technically accurate.
  - Enumerating all Cloud SQL MySQL instances is necessary and correct.

- **Minor Gaps:**  
  - The assessment does not specify how to enumerate only MySQL instances (as opposed to PostgreSQL or SQL Server). The `databaseVersion` property should be filtered for MySQL variants (e.g., `MYSQL_5_7`, `MYSQL_8_0`).
  - The program name could be more descriptive (see below).

- **Program Name:**  
  - `gcp_sql_instance_skip_show_database_flag_enabled` is clear, but could be improved for specificity (see Improvements).

---

### 3. IMPROVEMENTS

- **Enumerate Only MySQL Instances:**  
  The control applies only to MySQL. The automation should explicitly filter instances where `databaseVersion` starts with `MYSQL`.  
  Example:  
  ```bash
  gcloud sql instances list --format="json" | jq '.[] | select(.databaseVersion | startswith("MYSQL")) | .name'
  ```

- **API-Driven Alternative:**  
  For large environments, using the [Cloud SQL Admin API](https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/mysql/admin-api/rest/v1beta4/instances/list) with a script or security tool may be more efficient than CLI.

- **Program Name Suggestion:**  
  For clarity and convention, consider:  
  `gcp_sql_mysql_instance_skip_show_database_flag_on`  
  This makes it explicit that the check is for MySQL, not all SQL instances.

- **Edge Case Handling:**  
  - The check should handle the case where the flag is not set at all (should be a fail).
  - The script should report instances where the flag is missing, not just set to `off`.

- **Documentation:**  
  - Reference the CIS Benchmark section in the script or documentation for traceability.

---

### 4. CONFIDENCE: **HIGH**

- The assessment is technically sound and aligns with GCP API/SDK capabilities.
- Minor improvements are recommended, but the core approach is correct.

---

### 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: **Modify**

- **Modify** the automation to:
  - Explicitly filter for MySQL instances using `databaseVersion`.
  - Update the program name to `gcp_sql_mysql_instance_skip_show_database_flag_on` for clarity.
  - Ensure the check fails if the flag is missing, not just set to `off`.

- **No need to reclassify** the control; automated is correct.

---

**Summary Table**

| Section                | Verdict / Score / Recommendation              |
|------------------------|----------------------------------------------|
| Approach Validation    | AGREE                                        |
| Technical Accuracy     | 9/10                                         |
| Improvements           | See above                                    |
| Confidence             | HIGH                                         |
| Final Recommendation   | Modify (see improvements)                    |

---

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identified that the `skip_show_database` flag for Cloud SQL MySQL instances can be programmatically audited using the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`) and Cloud SQL Admin API. The flag is exposed in the `settings.databaseFlags` property, and all necessary information to determine compliance is available via API/CLI without manual inspection. The only minor improvement is to ensure the automation targets only MySQL instances, which is feasible by filtering on the `databaseVersion` property.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
gcp_sql_mysql_instance_skip_show_database_flag_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- GCP Resource/Service: Cloud SQL MySQL Instances
- API/CLI Method:  
  - List all Cloud SQL instances: `gcloud sql instances list --format=json`
  - For each instance, check `databaseVersion` starts with `MYSQL`
  - For each MySQL instance, describe instance: `gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> --format=json`
- Properties to Check:  
  - `.settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="skip_show_database") | .value` must be `"on"`
- Pass Criteria:  
  - Every Cloud SQL MySQL instance has the `skip_show_database` flag explicitly set to `"on"`
- Fail Criteria:  
  - Any Cloud SQL MySQL instance is missing the flag, or the flag is set to any value other than `"on"`

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
Both AI opinions agreed on automation, with the second opinion noting the importance of filtering for MySQL instances. This is incorporated into the final automation details for completeness and precision.

**SOURCE:** CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
